enums.name: MapsInfrastructureEncryption
enums.doc: (Optional) Discouraged to include in resource definition. Only needed where it is possible to disable platform (AKA infrastructure) encryption. Azure SQL TDE is an example of this. Values are enabled and disabled.

enums.name: PublicNetworkAccess
enums.doc: Property to specify whether the Maps Account will accept traffic from public internet. If set to 'disabled' all traffic except private endpoint traffic and that that originates from trusted services will be blocked.

enums.doc: The name of the SKU, in standard format (such as G2).

enums.name: MapsAccountKind
enums.doc: The Kind of the Maps Account.

enums.name: ManagedServiceIdentityType

values.name: SystemAssigned,UserAssigned
values.doc: System and user assigned managed identity.

enums.doc: Type of managed service identity (where both SystemAssigned and UserAssigned types are allowed).

enums.name: MapsSigningKey
enums.doc: The Maps account key to use for signing. Picking `primaryKey` or `secondaryKey` will use the Maps account Shared Keys, and using `managedIdentity` will use the auto-renewed private key to sign the SAS.

enums.name: MapsKeyType
enums.doc: Whether the operation refers to the primary or secondary key.

enums.name: ResourceProvisioningState
enums.doc: The provisioning state of a resource type.

enums.name: Origin

values.name: user
values.doc: Indicates the operation is initiated by a user.

values.name: system
values.doc: Indicates the operation is initiated by a system.

values.name: user,system
values.doc: Indicates the operation is initiated by a user or system.

enums.doc: The intended executor of the operation; as in Resource Based Access Control (RBAC) and audit logs UX. Default value is "user,system"

enums.name: ActionType
enums.doc: Extensible enum. Indicates the action type. "Internal" refers to actions that are for internal only APIs.

models.name: MapsAccount
models.doc: An Azure resource which represents access to a suite of Maps REST APIs.

baseModel.name: TrackedResource
baseModel.doc: The resource model definition for an Azure Resource Manager tracked top level resource which has 'tags' and a 'location'

baseModel.name: Resource
baseModel.doc: Common fields that are returned in the response for all Azure Resource Manager resources

properties.name: id
properties.doc: Fully qualified resource ID for the resource. Ex - /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/{resourceProviderNamespace}/{resourceType}/{resourceName}

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name of the resource

properties.name: type
properties.doc: The type of the resource. E.g. "Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines" or "Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts"

properties.name: systemData
properties.doc: Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.

type.name: SystemData
type.doc: Metadata pertaining to creation and last modification of the resource.

properties.name: createdBy
properties.doc: The identity that created the resource.

properties.name: createdByType
properties.doc: The type of identity that created the resource.

properties.name: createdAt
properties.doc: The timestamp of resource creation (UTC).

properties.name: lastModifiedBy
properties.doc: The identity that last modified the resource.

properties.name: lastModifiedByType
properties.doc: The type of identity that last modified the resource.

properties.name: lastModifiedAt
properties.doc: The timestamp of resource last modification (UTC)

properties.name: tags
properties.doc: Resource tags.

properties.name: location
properties.doc: The geo-location where the resource lives

properties.name: properties
properties.doc: The map account properties.

properties.name: uniqueId
properties.doc: A unique identifier for the Maps Account

properties.name: disableLocalAuth
properties.doc: Allows toggle functionality on Azure Policy to disable Azure Maps local authentication support. This will disable Shared Keys and Shared Access Signature Token authentication from any usage.

properties.name: provisioningState
properties.doc: The provisioning state of the Maps account resource, Account updates can only be performed on terminal states. Terminal states: `Succeeded` and `Failed`

properties.name: linkedResources
properties.doc: The array of associated resources to the Maps account. Linked resource in the array cannot individually update, you must update all linked resources in the array together. These resources may be used on operations on the Azure Maps REST API. Access is controlled by the Maps Account Managed Identity(s) permissions to those resource(s).

valueType.name: MapsLinkedResource
valueType.doc: Linked resource is reference to a resource deployed in an Azure subscription, add the linked resource `uniqueName` value as an optional parameter for operations on Azure Maps Geospatial REST APIs.

properties.name: uniqueName
properties.doc: A provided name which uniquely identifies the linked resource.

properties.name: id
properties.doc: ARM resource id in the form: '/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Storage/accounts/{storageName}'.

properties.name: cors
properties.doc: Specifies CORS rules for the Blob service. You can include up to five CorsRule elements in the request. If no CorsRule elements are included in the request body, all CORS rules will be deleted, and CORS will be disabled for the Blob service.

type.name: MapsCorsRules
type.doc: Sets the CORS rules. You can include up to five CorsRule elements in the request.

properties.name: corsRules
properties.doc: The list of CORS rules. You can include up to five CorsRule elements in the request.

valueType.name: MapsCorsRule
valueType.doc: Specifies a CORS rule for the Map Account.

properties.name: allowedOrigins
properties.doc: Required if CorsRule element is present. A list of origin domains that will be allowed via CORS, or "*" to allow all domains

properties.name: encryption
properties.doc: All encryption configuration for a resource.

type.name: MapsEncryption
type.doc: (Optional) Discouraged to include in resource definition. Only needed where it is possible to disable platform (AKA infrastructure) encryption. Azure SQL TDE is an example of this. Values are enabled and disabled.

properties.name: infrastructureEncryption
properties.doc: Values are enabled and disabled.

properties.name: customerManagedKeyEncryption
properties.doc: All Customer-managed key encryption properties for the resource.

type.name: CustomerManagedKeyEncryption
type.doc: Customer-managed key encryption properties for the resource.

properties.name: keyEncryptionKeyIdentity
properties.doc: All identity configuration for Customer-managed key settings defining which identity should be used to auth to Key Vault.

type.name: CustomerManagedKeyEncryptionKeyIdentity
type.doc: All identity configuration for Customer-managed key settings defining which identity should be used to auth to Key Vault.

properties.name: identityType
properties.doc: The type of identity to use. Values can be systemAssignedIdentity, userAssignedIdentity, or delegatedResourceIdentity.

properties.name: userAssignedIdentityResourceId
properties.doc: User assigned identity to use for accessing key encryption key Url. Ex: /subscriptions/fa5fc227-a624-475e-b696-cdd604c735bc/resourceGroups/<resource group>/providers/Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities/myId. Mutually exclusive with identityType systemAssignedIdentity.

properties.name: federatedClientId
properties.doc: application client identity to use for accessing key encryption key Url in a different tenant. Ex: f83c6b1b-4d34-47e4-bb34-9d83df58b540

properties.name: delegatedIdentityClientId
properties.doc: delegated identity to use for accessing key encryption key Url. Ex: /subscriptions/fa5fc227-a624-475e-b696-cdd604c735bc/resourceGroups/<resource group>/providers/Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities/myId. Mutually exclusive with identityType systemAssignedIdentity and userAssignedIdentity - internal use only.

properties.name: KeyEncryptionKeyUri
properties.doc: key encryption key Url, versioned or non-versioned. Ex: https://contosovault.vault.azure.net/keys/contosokek/562a4bb76b524a1493a6afe8e536ee78 or https://contosovault.vault.azure.net/keys/contosokek.

properties.name: locations
properties.doc: List of additional data processing regions for the Maps Account, which may result in requests being processed in another geography. Some features or results may be restricted to specific regions. By default, Maps REST APIs process requests according to the account location or the [geographic scope](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-maps/geographic-scope).

properties.name: locationName
properties.doc: The location name.

properties.name: privateEndpointConnections

A maximum of 15 tags can be provided for a resource. Each tag must have a key no greater than 128 characters and value no greater than 256 characters.

models.name: MapsAccountSasContent
models.doc: Parameters used to create an account Shared Access Signature (SAS) token. The REST API access control is provided by Azure Maps Role Based Access (RBAC) identity and access.

properties.name: signingKey
properties.doc: The Maps account key to use for signing. Picking `primaryKey` or `secondaryKey` will use the Maps account Shared Keys, and using `managedIdentity` will use the auto-renewed private key to sign the SAS.

properties.name: principalId
properties.doc: The principal Id also known as the object Id of a User Assigned Managed Identity currently assigned to the Maps Account. To assign a Managed Identity of the account, use operation Create or Update an assign a User Assigned Identity resource Id.

properties.name: regions
properties.doc: Optional, allows control of which region locations are permitted access to Azure Maps REST APIs with the SAS token. Example: "eastus", "westus2". Omitting this parameter will allow all region locations to be accessible.

properties.name: maxRatePerSecond
properties.doc: Required parameter which represents the desired maximum request per second to allowed for the given SAS token. This does not guarantee perfect accuracy in measurements but provides application safe guards of abuse with eventual enforcement.

properties.name: start
properties.doc: The date time offset of when the token validity begins. For example "2017-05-24T10:42:03.1567373Z". Maximum duration allowed is 24 hours between `start` and `expiry`.

properties.name: expiry
properties.doc: The date time offset of when the token validity expires. For example "2017-05-24T10:42:03.1567373Z". Maximum duration allowed is 24 hours between `start` and `expiry`.

models.name: MapsAccountSasToken
models.doc: A new Sas token which can be used to access the Maps REST APIs and is controlled by the specified Managed identity permissions on Azure (IAM) Role Based Access Control.

properties.name: accountSasToken
properties.doc: The shared access signature access token.

models.name: MapsAccountKeys
models.doc: The set of keys which can be used to access the Maps REST APIs. Two keys are provided for key rotation without interruption.

properties.name: PrimaryKeyLastUpdatedOn
properties.doc: The last updated date and time of the primary key.

properties.name: primaryKey
properties.doc: The primary key for accessing the Maps REST APIs.

properties.name: secondaryKey
properties.doc: The secondary key for accessing the Maps REST APIs.

properties.name: SecondaryKeyLastUpdatedOn
properties.doc: The last updated date and time of the secondary key.

models.name: MapsKeySpecification
models.doc: Whether the operation refers to the primary or secondary key.

properties.name: keyType
properties.doc: Whether the operation refers to the primary or secondary key.

methods.name: listSas
methods.doc:
Create and list an account shared access signature token. Use this SAS token for authentication to Azure Maps REST APIs through various Azure Maps SDKs. As prerequisite to create a SAS Token.

Prerequisites:
1. Create or have an existing User Assigned Managed Identity in the same Azure region as the account.
2. Create or update an Azure Maps account with the same Azure region as the User Assigned Managed Identity is placed.

operation.name: listSas
operation.doc:
Create and list an account shared access signature token. Use this SAS token for authentication to Azure Maps REST APIs through various Azure Maps SDKs. As prerequisite to create a SAS Token.

Prerequisites:
1. Create or have an existing User Assigned Managed Identity in the same Azure region as the account.
2. Create or update an Azure Maps account with the same Azure region as the User Assigned Managed Identity is placed.
